mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, equity discount rates compress, reducing the cost of capital and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. This mechanism operates through the direct channel of lower risk-free rates increasing present values of future corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-06-01 suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: In walk-forward testing, 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25pp are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-stat < 1.5), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which compresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2024-06-01 remains historically low but subject to upside surprises
failure_condition: Negative correlation between month-over-month UNRATE changes and 5-day forward SPX returns is not statistically significant (|correlation| < 0.08 over rolling 252-day windows), or SPX produces positive returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate profit expectations. When GDPC1 grows above its 2-year trend rate, equity valuations expand and S&P 500 produces positive returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as the market reprices growth upward and extends the expansion narrative.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate exceeds 2-year moving average by > 0.5pp annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23082.119 (revised) as of 2024-01-01 shows recent upward revision
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 50% of quarters following above-trend GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and housing investment, which together compress aggregate demand expectations. When both series decline simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 20 trading days as the market reprices cyclical growth downward.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 2% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1316.0 (revised) and RSAFS at 692774.0 (revised) as of 2024-05-01 show recent upward revisions suggesting demand resilience
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 2% in the same month, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward return direction on both proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend indicates strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. When INDPRO accelerates, corporate profit margins expand and S&P 500 produces positive returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings upward and extends the productivity narrative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate exceeds 12-month moving average by > 0.3pp
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.863 (revised) as of 2024-05-01 shows recent downward revision from 103.1168 vintage
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 50% of months following above-trend INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below the inflation rate (negative real rates), the central bank faces pressure to tighten. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate future rate hikes and earnings compression from higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 313.044 (revised) and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-06-01
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances, or the interaction term between inflation and real rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month, signaling labor market cooling and reduced wage pressure. This regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets interpret slowing employment as reducing inflation risk and increasing the probability of near-term monetary easing.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157695.0 (revised) as of 2024-06-01 shows recent downward revision from 158638.0 vintage
regime_hint: labor market cooling with disinflation expectations
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth falls below 100,000 jobs, 5-day forward SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates to below 2% year-over-year while unemployment remains below 4.5%, indicating consumer demand weakness despite tight labor markets. This divergence signals demand-side exhaustion and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward and anticipates margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 692774.0 (revised) and UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2024-06-01 show tight labor market with potential demand softness
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances, or the interaction coefficient between weak sales growth and low unemployment is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
